content.full_text:
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322·5662 
Administration 
322·5660 
Executive/Legal 
322·5901 
August 15, 1984 
Patty Yocom 
Office of Senator John Garamendi 
State Capitol, Room 313 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Enforcement 
322-6441 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-84-165 
Dear Ms. Yocom: 
This letter is to clarify our discussions concerning the 
campaign reporting required by the Friends of Frank Barbaro 
Committee and the Senate Majority Leader's Fund. 
The letter 
that I wrote to you on July 25, 1984, is no longer applicable. 
FACTS 
A fundraiser was held by the Friends of Frank Barbaro 
Committee to help to retire the Committee's campaign debt. 
Those attending the event were aware that it was held for 
Mr. Barbaro, but all of the checks were made out to the Senate 
Majority Leader's Fund, which is controlled by Senator 
Garamendi. 
A total of $5,000 in contributions were collected. 
In the case of three contributors, the Barbaro Committee 
promised that the amount they contributed ($1,800) would be used 
to partially payoff loans on which the contributors are 
guarantors. 
The remaining amount ($3,200) was divided between 
the Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee and the Senate Majority 
Leader's Fund. 
After depositing the checks, the Senate Majority 
Leader's Fund kept its $1,600 payment and forwarded a check to 
the Barbaro Committee for $3,400 ($1,800 to payoff the loans 
plus $1,600, which is the Committee's share of the $3,200 
remainder) • 
QUESTION PRESENTED 
Under the Political Reform Act, how should these 
transactions be reported? 


Patty Yocom 
August 15, 1984 
Page 2 
DISCUSSION 
The following describes, in detail, how the transactions 
must be reported: 
The Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee: 
On Schedule A (Contributions Received), the Committee will 
disclose the receipt of $3,200 in contributions ($5,000 total 
contributions minus the $1,800 which is to be used to repay the 
loans). 
Each contributor of $100 or more will be itemized. 
Following the itemization of these contributors, the Committee 
will disclose, in a lump-sum, any contributions of less than 
$100. 
After these entries, a notation should be made that: 
"These contributions were received by the Senate Majority 
Leader's Fund (the Fund's address and I.D. number) which was 
acting as an intermediary for the Barbaro Committee." 
On Schedule B, Part 2 (Loans Repaid, Forgiven or Paid by a 
Third Party), the Committee will disclose the three individuals 
or entities who provided the $1,800 which is to be used to 
partially repay the Committee's loans. 
The name and address of 
each contributor will be listed separately, along with the 
amount that the contributor provided for the repayment. 
After 
these entries, a notation should be made that: 
"These loan 
repayments were received through the Senate Majority Leader's 
Fund (the Fund's address and I.D. number) which was acting as an 
intermediary for the Barbaro Committee." 
On Schedule E (Payments and Contributions Made), the 
Committee will report that it made a $1,600 contribution to the 
Senate Majority Leader's Fund. 
The Senate Majority Leader's Fund: 
On Schedule G (Miscellaneous Adjustments to Cash position) , 
the Fund will show that it received $5,000 in contributions 
earmarked for the Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee. 
In a 
separate entry, it will report a $1,600 payment to the Barbaro 
Committee. 
(This $1,600 payment was not physically returned to 
the Barbaro Committee, but, rather, it was retained by the 
Senate Majority Leader's Fund as a contribution from the Barbaro 
Committee.) 
(See discussion below re Schedule A.) 
This second, 
Schedule G, entry is primarily for the purpose of making the 
Fund's records balance. 


Patty Yocom 
August 15, 1984 
Page 3 
On Schedule E (Expenditures), the Senate Majority Leader's 
Fund will report the $3,400 check that it wrote to the Barbaro 
Committee. 
On Schedule A (Contributions Received), the Senate Majority 
Leader's Fund will report the receipt of a $1,600 contribution 
from the Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee. 
If you have any questions about this advice, please feel 
free to contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
JSM:plh 
Very truly yours, 
~tuWs c:5Iian.k ~ 
Janis Shank McLean 
Staff Counsel 
Legal Division 


P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
John Hanna 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322·5662 
Administration 
322·5660 
August 15, 1984 
Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee 
517 N. Main, 2nd Floor 
Santa Ana, CA 
92701 
Executive/legal 
322·5901 
Enfarcement 
322·6441 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-84-165 
Dear Mr. Hanna: 
This letter is to clarify our discussions concerning the 
campaign reporting required by the Friends of Frank Barbaro 
Committee and the Senate Majority Leader's Fund. 
The letter 
that I wrote to you on July 25, 1984, is no longer applicable. 
FACTS 
A fundraiser was held by the Friends of Frank Barbaro 
Committee to help to retire the Committee's campaign debt. 
Those attending the event were aware that it was held for 
Mr. Barbaro, but all of the checks were made out to the Senate 
Majority Leader's Fund, which is controlled by Senator 
Garamendi. 
A total of $5,000 in contributions were collected. 
In the case of three contributors, the Barbaro Committee 
promised that the amount they contributed ($1,800) would be used 
to partially payoff loans on which the contributors are 
guarantors. 
The remaining amount ($3,200) was divided between 
the Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee and the Senate Majority 
Leader's Fund. 
After depositing the checks, the Senate Majority 
Leader's Fund kept its $1,600 payment and forwarded a check to 
the Barbaro Committee for $3,400 ($1,800 to payoff the loans 
plus $1,600, which is the Committee's share of the $3,200 
remainder) . 
QUESTION PRESENTED 
Under the Political Reform Act, how should these 
transactions be reported? 


John Hanna 
August 15, 1984 
Page 2 
DISCUSSION 
The following describes, in detail, how the transactions 
must be reported: 
The Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee: 
On Schedule A (Contributions Received), the Committee will 
disclose the receipt of $3,200 in contributions ($5,000 total 
contributions minus the $1,800 which is to be used to repay the 
loans). 
Each contributor of $100 or more will be itemized. 
Following the itemization of these contributors, the Committee 
will disclose, in a lump-sum, any contributions of less than 
$100. 
After these entries, a notation should be made that: 
"These contributions were received by the Senate Majority 
Leader's Fund (the Fund's address and 1.0. number) which was 
acting as an intermediary for the Barbaro Committee." 
On Schedule B, Part 2 (Loans Repaid, Forgiven or Paid by a 
Third Party), the Committee will disclose the three individuals 
or entities who provided the $1,800 which is to be used to 
partially repay the Committee's loans. 
The name and address of 
each contributor will be listed separately, along with the 
amount that the contributor provided for the repayment. 
After 
these entries, a notation should be made that: 
"These loan 
repayments were received through the Senate Majority Leader's 
Fund (the Fund's address and 1.0. number) which was acting as an 
intermediary for the Barbaro Committee." 
On Schedule E (Payments and Contributions Made), the 
Committee will report that it made a $1,600 contribution to the 
Senate Majority Leader's Fund. 
The Senate Majority Leader's Fund: 
On Schedule G (Miscellaneous Adjustments to Cash Position), 
the Fund will show that it received $5,000 in contributions 
earmarked for the Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee. 
In a 
separate entry, it will report a $1,600 payment to the Barbaro 
Committee. 
(This $1,600 payment was not physically returned to 
the Barbaro Committee, but, rather, it was retained by the 
Senate Majority Leader's Fund as a contribution from the Barbaro 
Committee.) 
(See discussion below re Schedule A.) 
This second, 
Schedule G, entry is primarily for the purpose of making the 
Fund's records balance. 


John Hanna 
August 15, 1984 
Page 3 
On Schedule E (Expenditures), the Senate Majority Leader's 
Fund will report the $3,400 check that it wrote to the Barbaro 
Committee. 
On Schedule A (Contributions Received), the Senate Majority 
Leader's Fund will report the receipt of a $1,600 contribution 
from the Friends of Frank Barbato Committee. 
If you have any questions about this advice, please feel 
free to contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
JSM:plh 
Very truly yours,

(916) 322·5662 
322·5660 
322·5901 
322-4441 
322~ 
July 25, 1984 
Mark Bite 
Office of Senator John Garamendi 
State Capitol, Room 313 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Bite: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice, 
Our Advice No. A-84-165 
Thank you for your request for advice on the campaign 
reporting provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
FACTS PRESENTED 
Last month a fundraiser was held to help retire the campaign 
debt of former Senate candidate Frank Barbaro. 
Those attending 
the event were aware that it was held for Mr. Barbaro, but all 
of the checks were made out to the "Senate Majority Leader's 
Fund," which is controlled by Senator Garamendi. 
Of the $5,000 
raised at the event, $3,400 will be sent to the "Friends of 
Frank Barbaro Committee," and the remaining $1,600 will stay in 
the Senate Majority Leader's Fund to cover the cost of the 
fundraiser. 
QUESTION PRESENTED 
Under the Political Reform Act, how should these 
transactions be reported? 
DISCUSSION 
The contributions received by the Senate Majority's 
Leadership Fund are "earmarked" contributions because, although 
they were received by the Fund, they were intended for the Frank 
Barbaro Committee. 
The Fund acted as an intermediary for the 
committee in receiving the funds. 
As an intermediary, the Fund 
must provide the Barbaro Committee with all of the information 
the Committee needs to disclose the required information 


Mark Hite 
July 25, 1984 
Page 2 
concerning the contributors, the Fund, and any expenditures made 
by the Fund. 
In making expenditures to vendors on behalf of the 
Barbaro Committee, the Senate Majority Leadership Fund acted as 
an "agent" for the Committee and the Committee' must report the 
payments to the vendors iri the same detail as if it had made the 
payments itself. 
The following describes, in detail, how the transactions 
must be reported: 
The Senate Majority Leader's Fund 
On Schedule G (Miscellaneous Adjustment to Cash Position), 
the Fund will show that it received S5,000 in contributions 
earmarked for the Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee. 
On Schedule E (Payments and Contributions Made), the Fund 
will show that a Sl,600 expenditure was made for a fundraiser 
for Frank Barbaro. 
The expenditures will be itemized on the 
campaign forms filed by the Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee, 
rather than on the forms filed by the Fund. 
However, the Fund 
will note, next to the Sl,600 lump-sum expenditure, that the 
details concerning the expenditure can be found on the forms 
filed by the Friends of Frank Barbaro. 
Schedule E will also 
state that the Fund gave the Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee 
$3,400 in earmarked funds that the Fund received. 
The Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee 
On Schedule A (Contributions Received), the Committee will 
state: 
"The following contributions were received through the 
Senate Majority Leader's Fund (the Fund's address and 1.0. 
number) which was acting as an intermediary .... " 
Following this 
explanatory note, each contributor of $100 or more will be 
itemized. 
Following the itemization of these contributors, the 
Committee will disclose, in a lump-sum, any contributions of 
less than SlOO. 
For the purpose of this disclosure, the 
contributions are considered to have been received on the date 
that they were received by the Fund, not the date they were 
received by the Barbaro Committee. 
The Senate Majority Leader's 
Fund must provide the Committee with the information needed for 
this disclosure, i.e., the name, address, occupation and 
employer of each contributor, and the amount contributed. 
On Schedule E (Payments and Contributions), the Committee 
will state that a payment of Sl,600 was made to the Senate 


Mark Hite 
July 25, 1984 
Page 3 
Majority Leader's Fund "in order to allow the Fund to pay the 
following vendors .... " 
The expenditures to each vendor will 
then be itemized in the manner described in the enclosed copy of 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18431. 
(This regulation was approved 
by the Commission and is currently receiving expedited review by 
the Office of Administrative Law.) 
Any expenditures which are 
not required to be itemized may be reported in a lump-sum. 
The 
Senate Majority Leader's Fund must provide the Friends of Prank 
Barbaro Committee with the information needed for this 
disclosure, i.e., the name and address of the payee, the 
description of the goods or services received, and the amount of 
the expenditure. 
. 
If I can be of any additional help to you, please feel free 
to contact me at 916/322-5901. 
JSM:km 
Enclosure 
Very truly yours, 
!JtuUi5 8~~i!AJV 
Janis Shank McLean 
Counsel, Legal Division 


\::. P' P C 
~ .. -Tahn I r.,aramendi 
JUM "EMIft ~9dR1TY LEADER 
June 12, 1984 
Ms. Carla Wardlow 
Fair PoliticaL Practices 
Commission 
1.1.00 If.KIf. Street 
Sacramento, CA 
Dear Carla: 
Pursuant to our conversation this afternoon, I would like to 
have further clarification as to the Prop. 9 reporting 
requirements for the funds we received for former Senate 
candidate Frank Barbaro. 
As I mentioned this morning, last month a'iuridraiser was held 
to help retire Mr. Barbaro's campaign debt. 
Those who attended 
knew the event was for Barbaro but checks were made out to the 
"Senate Majority Leader's Fund" which is controlled by Senator 
Gararnendi. 
Of the $5,000 that was raised at this event, $3,400 
will be sent to the "Friends of Frank Barbaro" with the remaining 
$1,600 staying in the Senate Majority Leader's Fund. 
I would appreciate specific direction from the Commission as 
to how these monies are to be reported, both by Senator Garamendi 
and by Frank Barbaro. 
I appreciate your willingness to assist in 
this matter. 
MH/py 
Sincerely, 
MARK HITE 
State Capitol, Sacramento, California 95814 
NOT PRINTED AT STATE EXPENSE

sections.question:
Under the Political Reform Act, how should these 
transactions be reported? 


Patty Yocom 
August 15, 1984
sections.conclusion: The document provides detailed reporting instructions across multiple schedules for both the Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee and the Senate Majority Leader's Fund, requiring itemization of contributions of $100 or more, notation of intermediary status, separate reporting of loan repayments, and specification of payments made.

Patty Yocom

On Schedule E (Expenditures), the Senate Majority Leader's 
Fund will report the $3,400 check that it wrote to the Barbaro 
Committee. 
On Schedule A (Contributions Received), the Senate Majority 
Leader's Fund will report the receipt of a $1,600 contribution 
from the Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee.
sections.question_synthetic: How should contributions received by the Senate Majority Leader's Fund that are earmarked for the Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee, including loan repayments and a fundraiser cost retention, be reported under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that the Friends of Frank Barbaro Committee must report $3,200 in contributions on Schedule A with notation of intermediary status, $1,800 in loan repayments on Schedule B Part 2, and a $1,600 contribution to the Senate Majority Leader's Fund on Schedule E. The Senate Majority Leader's Fund must report the $5,000 earmarked contributions on Schedule G, the $3,400 payment to the Barbaro Committee on Schedule E, and the $1,600 contribution received from the Barbaro Committee on Schedule A.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, old; LLM: Document consists of three versions of the same advice letter (dated July 25 and August 15, 1984) sent to different recipients (Mark Bite/Hite, Patty Yocom, and John Hanna). The July 25 letter is superseded by the August 15 letters. All versions present identical FACTS and QUESTION sections with identical DISCUSSION guidance. High confidence in extraction due to clear structure and verbatim question/conclusion sections.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter A-84-165 addresses the proper Political Reform Act reporting for a fundraiser held for Frank Barbaro where contributions were made payable to Senator Garamendi's Senate Majority Leader's Fund, which acted as an intermediary and retained funds to cover event costs. The letter provides detailed schedules and notation requirements for both committees to accurately disclose the earmarked contributions and third-party loan repayments.
